questions.q: In which year did Annie Besant launch her Home Rule League in India?
questions.explanation: Annie Besant founded her Home Rule League in September 1916 to demand self-government for India.

questions.q: Annie Besant became the first woman president of which Indian organization in 1917?
questions.explanation: In 1917 Annie Besant became the first woman to preside over the Indian National Congress, at its Calcutta session.

questions.q: In which country was Annie Besant born?
questions.explanation: Annie Besant was born in 1847 in Clapham, London, England, though she had Irish ancestry on her mother's side.

questions.q: Annie Besant was a leading figure in which spiritual movement?
questions.explanation: Annie Besant was a prominent theosophist and became president of the Theosophical Society.

questions.q: What political demand did Annie Besant's Home Rule League primarily campaign for?
questions.explanation: The Home Rule League demanded self-government, or home rule, for India within the British Empire.

questions.q: Annie Besant spent much of her later life in which southern Indian city, headquarters of the Theosophical Society?
questions.explanation: Annie Besant was closely associated with Madras (Chennai), where the Theosophical Society had its headquarters at Adyar.

questions.q: Which English-language newspaper did Annie Besant use to spread her Home Rule ideas?
questions.explanation: Annie Besant founded and edited the newspaper New India to promote the Home Rule cause.

questions.q: Annie Besant's Home Rule League was one of two such leagues; the other was founded by which leader?
questions.explanation: Bal Gangadhar Tilak founded a separate Home Rule League in 1916, operating alongside Annie Besant's league.

questions.q: Annie Besant was a campaigner for the rights of which group before coming to India?
questions.explanation: Before settling in India, Besant was a social reformer in Britain who campaigned for workers' rights and women's causes.

questions.q: Annie Besant helped establish which educational institution at Banaras?
questions.explanation: Besant founded the Central Hindu College at Banaras in 1898, which later became part of Banaras Hindu University.

questions.q: In which year was Annie Besant born?
questions.explanation: Annie Besant was born on 1 October 1847 in London.

questions.q: Annie Besant succeeded which person as president of the Theosophical Society?
questions.explanation: Besant became president of the Theosophical Society in 1907, succeeding Colonel Henry Steel Olcott.

questions.q: In 1917 the British government in Madras took what action against Annie Besant over her Home Rule activity?
questions.explanation: Besant was interned by the Madras government in June 1917, which sparked widespread protest and boosted the Home Rule cause.

questions.q: Annie Besant's internment in 1917 contributed to which official British declaration later that year?
options: The Montagu Declaration on responsible government
questions.explanation: The pressure from her internment helped prompt Secretary of State Montagu's August 1917 declaration promising the gradual development of self-governing institutions.

questions.q: Which young Indian future leader presided over the Theosophical Society's Order of the Star, a movement Besant promoted?
questions.explanation: Besant promoted Jiddu Krishnamurti as a World Teacher and head of the Order of the Star in the East, though he later dissolved it.

questions.q: Before her theosophical years, Annie Besant was closely associated with which British social reformer in promoting birth control and freethought?
questions.explanation: Besant worked with the secularist Charles Bradlaugh and was famously tried with him over a birth-control publication in 1877.

questions.q: At which Congress session did Annie Besant become president in 1917?
questions.explanation: Annie Besant presided over the Calcutta session of the Indian National Congress in December 1917.

questions.q: Annie Besant's Home Rule League was particularly strong in which regions of India?
options: Madras Presidency and parts of the south and west
questions.explanation: Besant's league was especially active in the Madras Presidency and other areas outside Tilak's western Indian base.

questions.q: Annie Besant first arrived in India in which decade to work with the Theosophical Society?
questions.explanation: Besant came to India in 1893, embarking on the work that made Adyar her long-term home.

questions.q: Which children's and youth scouting-style organization did Besant help promote in India?
questions.explanation: Besant helped found an Indian Boy Scouts association at a time when the official scouting movement excluded Indian boys.

questions.q: Annie Besant translated and wrote commentary on which Hindu scripture, reflecting her devotion to Indian thought?
questions.explanation: Besant produced a well-known English translation of the Bhagavad Gita, reflecting her deep engagement with Hindu philosophy.

questions.q: Annie Besant's relationship with the Congress cooled after 1918 partly because she opposed which leader's emerging methods?
questions.explanation: Besant grew critical of Gandhi's non-cooperation programme, favouring constitutional methods, which distanced her from the Congress mainstream.

questions.q: In what year did Annie Besant die?
questions.explanation: Annie Besant died on 20 September 1933 at Adyar, Madras.

questions.q: Annie Besant drafted a proposed constitution for India known by what name in 1925?
questions.explanation: Besant promoted the Commonwealth of India Bill, an effort to frame a self-government constitution presented in the British Parliament in the mid-1920s.

questions.q: Annie Besant's surname came from her marriage to which clergyman, from whom she later separated?
questions.explanation: She married the Anglican clergyman Frank Besant in 1867 and retained the surname after their separation.

questions.q: During the 1888 London matchgirls' strike, what role did Annie Besant play?
options: She publicized their conditions and supported them
questions.explanation: Besant exposed the poor conditions of the Bryant and May matchgirls and helped support their famous 1888 strike.

questions.q: Annie Besant joined the Theosophical Society after reviewing a book by which founder of the movement?
questions.explanation: Besant was drawn to theosophy after being asked to review Helena Blavatsky's 'The Secret Doctrine' in 1889.

questions.q: The two Home Rule Leagues of 1916 are sometimes credited with reviving nationalist activity during which broader global event?
questions.explanation: The Home Rule Leagues energized Indian politics during the First World War, when many leaders were otherwise quiescent.

questions.q: Besant's New India faced repeated action from authorities involving which kind of legal demand?
questions.explanation: The colonial government repeatedly demanded security deposits from New India under press regulations, pressuring the paper.

questions.q: Annie Besant championed which broad principle in education that she tried to embody at the Central Hindu College?
options: A blend of Indian religious and ethical teaching with modern subjects
questions.explanation: Besant's Central Hindu College aimed to combine modern education with grounding in Hindu religious and moral values.
